campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they honored K9 Lucas, a police dog who recently passed away. The council also approved an updated beekeeping ordinance, discussed a state bill about development near transit areas (with some council members stepping back due to conflicts of interest), and reviewed a study about potentially creating homeless services in the West Valley region.
keyTopics: Memorial for K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
State transit-oriented development bill (SB 79) discussed with some council recusals
West Valley homeless services feasibility study moving toward regional task force
Annual city financial report accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met with five different community commissions to review and approve their plans for the next fiscal year. The commissions covered important areas like preserving historic buildings, running the library and public art programs, managing parks and recreation, improving traffic safety, and overseeing development and planning. The council also held a private meeting to discuss a legal case the city is pursuing.
keyTopics: Heritage Preservation Commission priorities for historic site protection
Library and Public Art Commission programming and initiatives
Parks and Recreation Commission budget and facility plans
Traffic Safety Commission projects and traffic improvement goals
Planning Commission development and zoning priorities for next year

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle routine business including approving meeting notes, reviewing the city's finances, and completing the weed removal program for the year. The council also adopted new rules for how they conduct meetings, approved raising flags for Pride Month and Juneteenth celebrations, and discussed plans for adding more affordable housing in the community.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completed for 2026
New affordable housing transfer rules adopted
Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
City council procedures and norms updated
Housing element implementation schedule being planned

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026 to approve meeting records, review the city's financial health from January, and move forward with several projects including a new residential development called Solana. The council also discussed plans for community health and senior services for the year ahead.
keyTopics: Solana Project residential development gets final approval
January 2026 financial and investment report reviewed
Annual progress check on housing and general development plans
2026 community health and senior services programs outlined
Updates to how city council meetings are documented and formatted

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a procedural meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for April 7 and April 14, 2026. The council reviewed which items to add to each agenda, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked legal notice requirements. They also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized April 7 meeting agenda with any new items and time assignments
Reviewed draft agenda for April 14 meeting
Checked legal notice requirements for agenda items and documents
Reviewed public record from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026, where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for residents to share comments and concerns. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Schedule for upcoming board and commission meetings
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that funds community development and housing programs in Sunnyvale. The commission approved meeting minutes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to help residents with housing and services.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding available for housing and community development projects
Review of applications from organizations seeking CDBG grants
Approval of previous meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and thrive. They discussed programs to encourage shop owners to get proper licenses, promoted a summer campaign to encourage residents to shop locally, and explored ways to partner with private companies to sponsor community events and offer incentives for retail stores and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get proper licenses
Summer Shop Local Campaign - encouraging residents to support local retailers
City Event Sponsorships - partnering with private companies to fund community events
Economic Incentives for Small Businesses and Startups - offering support to new and growing companies
Shopping Center Outreach - directly reaching out to businesses at local malls and centers

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Historical and Landmarks Commission held a hearing on April 2, 2026 to discuss historic buildings and sites in the city. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submissions by email before noon on meeting day
Historic preservation discussions for Santa Clara

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on March 17, 2026 to handle routine administrative business, including approving previous meeting minutes and reviewing the calendar for upcoming meetings. The council also approved travel for Mayor Montano to attend the WELL 2026 conference and confirmed a school district representative appointment to an economic committee.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming meeting dates
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
New appointment confirmed for Milpitas Unified School District representative to Economic Committee
Council reviewed requests for future agenda items from councilmembers

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to handle several routine matters including officially recognizing March as Women's History Month. The council approved an agreement allowing the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract to buy email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
